nodes.description: AIRE expression is absent from most thymomas, reducing tissue-restricted self-antigen display in the thymic epithelial compartment.
nodes.id: AIRE loss in thymic epithelial cells

evidence.reference_title: Common cellular and diverse genetic basis of thymoma-associated myasthenia gravis: role of MHC class II and AIRE genes and genetic polymorphisms.
evidence.snippet: Recently, we also found that expression of the autoimmune regulator (AIRE) gene is absent from approximately 95% of thymomas.

nodes.description: The cortex-dominant, medulla-poor thymoma microenvironment generates autoreactive effector T cells and underproduces regulatory T cells, allowing self-reactive T-cell escape.
nodes.id: Central tolerance failure in the thymic microenvironment

evidence.reference_title: Pathogenesis of thymoma-associated myasthenia gravis: a narrative review.
evidence.snippet: Since thymomas are mainly composed of the cortex, with few medullae, MG may be caused by immature thymoma-derived T cells that fail to undergo negative selection and have not yet acquired sufficient self-tolerance.

evidence.reference_title: Common cellular and diverse genetic basis of thymoma-associated myasthenia gravis: role of MHC class II and AIRE genes and genetic polymorphisms.
evidence.snippet: Generation of autoreactive CD4(+) effector T cells and defective production of regulatory CD4(+) T cells inside thymomas contribute to the development of myasthenia gravis (MG) in >90% of MG(+) thymomas.

evidence.reference_title: A specific missense mutation in GTF2I occurs at high frequency in thymic epithelial tumors.
evidence.snippet: In a series of 274 TETs, we detected the GTF2I mutation in 82% of type A and 74% of type AB thymomas but rarely in the aggressive subtypes, where recurrent mutations of known cancer genes have been identified.

nodes.description: Recurrent GTF2I mutation defines a major molecular facet of type A and AB thymoma and promotes thymic epithelial proliferation and transformation.

evidence.reference_title: A specific missense mutation in GTF2I occurs at high frequency in thymic epithelial tumors.
evidence.snippet: In a series of 274 TETs, we detected the GTF2I mutation in 82% of type A and 74% of type AB thymomas but rarely in the aggressive subtypes, where recurrent mutations of known cancer genes have been identified.

evidence.reference_title: Mutant GTF2I induces cell transformation and metabolic alterations in thymic epithelial cells.
evidence.snippet: Our findings identify GTF2I mutation as a new oncogenic driver that is responsible for transformation of thymic epithelial cells.

nodes.description: Thymoma-associated immunodeficiency with hypogammaglobulinemia and increased susceptibility to infection.

evidence.reference_title: Good syndrome combined with multiple microbial pulmonary infections: case report and review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: Good syndrome (GS), a rare acquired immunodeficiency disorder characterized by thymoma and hypogammaglobulinemia, predisposes individuals to recurrent infections.

nodes.description: Mutant GTF2I rewires glycolytic and stress-response programs, supporting tumor growth and survival under nutrient and DNA-damage stress.

evidence.reference_title: Mutant GTF2I induces cell transformation and metabolic alterations in thymic epithelial cells.
evidence.snippet: Gtf2i L424H knockin cells exhibited cell transformation, aneuploidy, and increase tumor growth and survival under glucose deprivation or DNA damage.

evidence.reference_title: Mutant GTF2I induces cell transformation and metabolic alterations in thymic epithelial cells.
evidence.snippet: Gtf2i mutation also increased the expression of several glycolytic enzymes, cyclooxygenase-2, and caused modifications of lipid metabolism.

nodes.description: The dominant autoimmune complication of thymoma, reflecting failure of central tolerance and neuromuscular autoantigen presentation.

evidence.snippet: Thymomas, in particular, can be associated to auto-immune disorders, among which predominates myasthenia gravis.

nodes.description: Myasthenia gravis-associated thymomas contain neuromuscular antigen-expressing medullary thymic epithelial populations that organize a local immune niche.

evidence.reference_title: Pathogenesis of thymoma-associated myasthenia gravis: a narrative review.
evidence.snippet: By analyzing bulk RNA-sequencing (RNA-seq) and single-cell RNA-seq (scRNA-seq) data from thymomas, we identified neuromuscular medullary thymic epithelial cells (nmTECs) as neuromuscular antigen-expressing cell populations.

evidence.reference_title: Pathogenesis of thymoma-associated myasthenia gravis: a narrative review.
evidence.snippet: We observed spatial nmTEC colocalization and an immune niche, inferring an interaction and suggesting a pathological role of nmTECs in MG.

nodes.description: Immune-mediated suppression of erythroid precursors causes severe anemia in a subset of thymoma patients.

evidence.reference_title: Management of Autoimmune Conditions Associated With Thymic Epithelial Tumors.
evidence.snippet: Myasthenia gravis is the most common, followed by less frequent but notable conditions including pure red cell aplasia, Good's syndrome, and various neurologic, dermatologic, and systemic autoimmune disorders.

nodes.description: Many thymomas, especially type A and AB tumors, show reduced MHC class II gene expression, weakening intrathymic antigen presentation.
nodes.id: Reduced MHC class II antigen presentation

biological_processes: antigen processing and presentation of peptide antigen via MHC class II

evidence.snippet: We report here that a) expression levels of class II major histocompatibility complex (MHCII) genes are variably decreased in thymomas, most prominently in histological WHO types A and AB;
